expectations.evidence: Both analysis.md and architecture.md exist with substantial content.

expectations.text: A1: Handles unmapped characteristics (workflow, configurability have no direct matrix rating) — either uses mapping/proxy or explicitly acknowledges the gap
expectations.evidence: No acknowledgment that workflow and configurability are unmapped characteristics. The analysis evaluates styles against these characteristics using subjective High/Medium/Low ratings without noting that they lack standard matrix ratings. No mapping or proxy approach is documented.

expectations.text: A2: Shows ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles (or at least 6+)
expectations.evidence: Only evaluates 5 styles in the comparison table: Modular Monolith, Service-Based, Microservices, Event-Driven, Space-Based. Missing Layered, Microkernel, and Service-Oriented.

expectations.text: A3: Reasonable recommendation given the characteristics (Service-Oriented, Microservices, or Service-Based are defensible choices)
expectations.evidence: Recommends Service-Based Architecture with event-driven augmentation. 'Primary style: Service-Based Architecture, augmented with event-driven patterns.' This is a defensible choice.
No $ symbols or explicit cost comparison. The word 'cost' does not appear in the analysis. No cost category is assigned to any style. Cost is not a factor in the recommendation.

expectations.text: A5: architecture.md has structured selection section
expectations.evidence: architecture.md has 'Selected Architecture Style' with Rationale, Key Structural Decisions, and Trade-offs Accepted. But there is NO evolution path section, and no cost category or partitioning type specified.
